fact: September 18 sits exactly 3 days before the autumn equinox, when day and night become perfectly equal twins!
In the Northern Hemisphere, leaves are secretly starting their color-changing magic right now, even if you can't see it yet.
Monarch butterflies are typically in the middle of their epic 3,000-mile journey south around this date, and some might be flying over your head today!
This date falls in the middle of Virgo season, making it one of the most "perfectionist" days of the year according to astrology fans.
September 18 is day number 261 of the year, leaving exactly 104 days until New Year's Eve - that's almost exactly one-third of a year left!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, spring flowers are bursting open right now while the north gets ready for fall.
The sun is setting about 2-3 minutes earlier each day around this time, and your shadow at noon is getting noticeably longer.
Squirrels in northern climates are in full panic mode right now, frantically burying nuts and forgetting where they put half of them.
September 18 lands in the peak of Atlantic hurricane season, when ocean waters are at their warmest and stormiest.
Apple trees in northern regions are heavy with ripe fruit right now, and the smell of fallen apples fills orchards with a sweet, cidery perfume.
Crickets are chirping their loudest around this date - you can actually tell the temperature by counting their chirps and doing simple math!
When there's a full moon on September 18, it's often called a Harvest Moon and looks extra huge and orange on the horizon.
